markets.question: Will Meituan have the best Coding AI model at the end of May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the company that owns the model that has the highest arena rank based on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) when the table under the "Leaderboard" tab for "Coding" is checked on May 31, 2026, 12:00 PM ET.

Results from the "Rank" column under the "Text Arena | Coding" Leaderboard tab at https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/coding-no-style-control with style control off will be used to resolve this market.

Models will be ordered primarily by their leaderboard rank at the market’s check time. If two or more models are tied on rank, they will be ordered by their Arena score, including any underlying, unrounded, granular values reflected in the data below the leaderboard. If a tie still remains, alphabetical order of company names as listed in this market group will be used as a final tiebreaker (e.g., if the two models are tied by exact arena score, “Google” would be ranked ahead of “xAI”). This market will resolve based on the company that occupies first place under this ranking.

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source is unavailable at check time, this market will remain open until the leaderboard comes back online and will resolve based on the first check after it becomes available. If it becomes permanently unavailable, this market will resolve based on another resolution source.

markets.question: Will "Pinocchio" be said on ICEMAN?
markets.description:
Drake is expected to release his new album "ICEMAN" in 2026.

This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed term is mentioned by anyone on the specified transcript for any track on the officially released full "ICEMAN" album. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Officially released means that Iceman is officially available for download or streaming (not including live events) by the resolution date. Any Drake album officially confirmed to be the Iceman project will count, regardless of potential name changes.

Samples or clips of audio where people are speaking or singing which are included in the album will qualify toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript. AI-generated audio will count toward this market's resolution so long as it is included in the specified transcript.

In the case a term is censored on a relevant transcript, audio may be consulted to verify that a qualifying mention occurred.

Misspellings or iterations of the listed term, including all grammatical or slang forms, or misspellings with extra, missing, or incorrect letters (ex: helloooooooo or heoll, for ‘hello’), will not count toward a “Yes” resolution, regardless of context or intent.

Any usage of the term, regardless of context, will count toward the resolution of this market.

Plural and possessive forms of the listed term will count toward the resolution of this market regardless of context; however, other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

This market will resolve according to the initial release of the full album on a qualifying streaming platform. Releases consisting only of individual songs or remixes released later will not qualify.

The resolution source for this market will be the audio transcripts for this album provided by the listed streaming platforms in the following order of priority: Spotify, Apple Music, YouTube Music, Amazon Music, Tidal (e.g., Spotify will be used unless it does not provide a necessary transcript, then Apple Music will be used, etc.).

If no such album is released in full by by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to based on what has been released up to that point. If the album is not released on any of the listed platforms by the specified end date, this market will resolve to "No".

markets.question: Will the Republican Party win the NY-08 House seat?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the party of the candidate who wins the NY-08 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The midterm elections will take place on November 4, 2026.

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Kuala Lumpur be 35°C or higher on May 10?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 10 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Kuala Lumpur Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/my/sepang-district/WMKK.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.description:
This is a polymarket to predict which clubs finish in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings for the 2025–26 season.

If the listed club officially finishes in the top 4 of the English Premier League standings at the end of the season, the market will resolve to "Yes." Otherwise, it will resolve to "No."

If two or more clubs are tied on points for a top 4 position, resolution will follow the official English Premier League tiebreaking procedures.

If the 2025–26 English Premier League season is canceled or not completed before October 1, 2026, this market will resolve to "No."

The primary resolution source will be official information from the English Premier League. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Arsenal and Manchester City have secured top-four Premier League finishes and Champions League qualification, leading the table with 76 and 74 points after 35 matches, their superior goal differences (+41, +40) providing crucial buffers. Manchester United sit third on 65 points from 36 games, but the fourth spot remains fiercely contested: Liverpool hold a slim one-point edge over Aston Villa (59-58 pts), with Villa benefiting from a game in hand and both sides having two matches left amid tight scheduling. Bournemouth (55 pts) and Brighton (53 pts) lurk as outside threats. Liverpool's push is complicated by injury doubts for Mohamed Salah (thigh) and Alisson Becker, while remaining fixtures—including potential rival head-to-heads and relegation battles—will dictate the final standings in this high-stakes race for Europe's elite competition.

markets.question: Will Solana dip to $20 by December 31, 2026?

Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No.”

The resolution source for this market is Binance, specifically the SOL/USDT “Low” prices available at:

markets.question: Joel Embiid: Points O/U 25.5
markets.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Joel Embiid scores more than 25.5 points during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if Joel Embiid scores 25.5 points or fewer during the game.

The entire game including all overtime periods will be considered.

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.

If the player is listed as inactive or otherwise does not take the court at any point, the market will resolve "No".

The resolution source will be the official NBA box score as published on NBA.com.

events.title: Knicks vs. 76ers
events.description:
In the upcoming NBA game, scheduled for May 10 at 3:30PM ET:
If the Knicks win, the market will resolve to "Knicks".
If the 76ers win, the market will resolve to "76ers".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50-50.
The result will be determined based on the final score including any overtime periods.

eventMetadata.context_description: The New York Knicks hold a commanding 3-0 lead in their Eastern Conference semifinals series against the Philadelphia 76ers after a 108-94 Game 3 road win on May 8, powered by Jalen Brunson's 33 points and dominant rebounding with 13 offensive boards for 20-plus second-chance points. Despite OG Anunoby missing Game 3 with a right hamstring strain sustained late in Game 2—he's now questionable for Sunday's Game 4—New York's defense clamped down late, extending their playoff win streak to six. Joel Embiid returned in Game 3 but is probable for Game 4 amid right hip soreness, while the desperate 76ers rely on Tyrese Maxey amid home-court urgency to stave off a sweep. Knicks' momentum and depth shape trader consensus.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment for tech IPOs before 2027 hinges on recent confidential S-1 filings reported for SpaceX—targeting a $2 trillion valuation in June-July 2026—and Discord, alongside preparations by Databricks amid 65% revenue growth to $4 billion. Stripe's $159 billion liquidity deal signals readiness despite no formal filing, while OpenAI and Anthropic advance legal groundwork for second-half listings, fueled by AI infrastructure demand. Improving market conditions post-2025 rebound, with Cerebras Systems eyeing a May debut, bolster consensus on a 2026 wave. Key catalysts include S-1 public disclosures, roadshows, and earnings beats; delays from regulatory scrutiny or volatility remain risks.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices a 68.5% implied probability for a SpaceX IPO in June, driven by the company's confidential S-1 filing with the SEC on April 1, 2026, and subsequent leaks detailing a late-May public prospectus, roadshow starting the week of June 8, and pricing around June 15 at a targeted $1.75–$2 trillion valuation with a $75 billion raise. Banker briefings led by Elon Musk and CFO Bret Johnsen in early April outlined unprecedented 30% retail share allocation via lead underwriters Morgan Stanley, Goldman Sachs, and others, while accelerated employee stock vesting underscores momentum. August at 16.4% reflects potential regulatory or market delays, with Starlink's projected $22–24 billion 2026 revenue bolstering fundamentals amid Nasdaq 100 eligibility post-listing. Key watch: S-1 release and quiet period dynamics.

markets.question: Will Ukraine re-enter Obratne or Temyrivka by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if, according to the ISW map, Ukraine captures any territory of Obratne (47.827331° N, 36.548744° E) or Temyrivka (47.825114° N, 36.570672° E), Zaporizhzhya Oblast, between market creation and the specified date (ET).

Territory will be considered captured if any part of the specified territory is shaded under a below specified layer on the ISW map (https://storymaps.arcgis.com/stories/36a7f6a6f5a9448496de641cf64bd375) by the resolution date. Otherwise, the market will resolve to “No”.

For any change on the ISW map to qualify for this market’s resolution, the relevant shading indicating Ukrainian control must persist through the next full ISW daily update cycle. If ISW skips a day, shading must persist until the next finalized ISW update is published, regardless of the date. Any continuous shading which reflects either "Reported Ukrainian Territorial Gains in the Past 24 Hours", or “Claimed Ukrainian Counteroffensives” will qualify. 

Once a qualifying condition is met, any subsequent loss of control will not be considered towards the resolution of this market.

If Ukraine comes into control of the specified territory as a result of a negotiated settlement, this will qualify for a 'Yes' resolution, regardless of whether it is shaded blue in the ISW map. However, an announcement of a negotiated settlement that gives Ukraine de jure control will not qualify. Actual control must be established.

The primary resolution source for this market will be the ISW Ukraine map. If the ISW map is rendered unavailable, information from DeepStateMap (https://deepstatemap.live/) may be used. If information from both the ISW and DeepStateMap are rendered permanently unavailable, a consensus of credible reporting may be used.

Note: Any temporary glitches or errors in the map will not be considered.

eventMetadata.context_description: Russian forces captured the small frontline villages of Obratne and Temyrivka southeast of Oleksandrivka in Zaporizhzhia Oblast around mid-March 2026, maintaining control per ISW maps through early May amid ongoing attritional fighting. Ukrainian counteroffensives in the Oleksandrivka sector gained momentum in late April, liberating adjacent settlements including Novohryhorivka, Kalynivske, Stepove, Ternove, Vorone, and Sichneve, while precision strikes targeted Russian ground lines of communication in occupied Zaporizhia. ISW assessments note Russian net territorial losses in April for the first time since August 2024, reflecting intensified Ukrainian resistance. Traders monitor daily ISW updates for any confirmed advances into these coordinates, with potential for further counterattacks to shift the dynamic before key resolution dates.

eventMetadata.context_description: Michael B. Jordan and Bad Bunny command trader consensus at 23.9% and 23.5% implied probabilities for People's Sexiest Man Alive 2026, fueled by Jordan's fresh awards-season momentum from his Best Actor Oscar win for Sinners at the March 2026 ceremony and standout red-carpet looks, contrasted with Bad Bunny's surging global music dominance and crossover appeal amid recent chart-topping releases. Close behind, breakout stars Connor Storrie and Hudson Williams at 9.5% and 9.0% ride viral buzz from their steamy Heated Rivalry photo shoots and promotional campaigns, positioning them as Gen Z disruptors against Hollywood heavyweights like Ryan Gosling and Timothée Chalamet. With People editors' opaque selection process historically favoring timely cultural icons, summer premieres and social media traction could swing the closely contested field before the typical November reveal.

markets.question: Will no company have an AI model hit 1550 on Chatbot Arena in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed entity, which is the first to reach an Arena Score of 1550+ on the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard (https://lmarena.ai/) by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. 

Results from the "Text Arena" section on the leaderboard/text tab of https://lmarena.ai/ with the style control unchecked (https://arena.ai/leaderboard/text/overall-no-style-control) will be used to resolve this market.

If no company's model reaches 1550+ Arena Score by the specified time, this market will resolve to "None in 2026".

If the first model to reach a 1550+ Arena Score belongs to any company other than the listed ones, this market will be resolved to "Other".

The resolution source for this market is the Chatbot Arena LLM Leaderboard found at https://lmarena.ai/. If this resolution source becomes unavailable, the market will remain open until it is accessible again. If it becomes permanently unavailable, resolution will be based on another credible source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no AI model reaching 1550 Elo on the Chatbot Arena leaderboard in 2026 (58.5% implied probability), as the highest scores hover around 1500–1504 amid diminishing returns in large language model scaling. Anthropic leads alternatives at 25.5% following its late February Claude Opus 4.6 release, which claimed the top spot across all LMSYS Chatbot Arena leaderboards—including a record 1504 in high-Elo battles and 1549 in coding—outpacing rivals through superior reasoning and instruction-following. Google trails at 14.5% with competitive Gemini 3.1 Pro (1476 Elo), while OpenAI's GPT-5.4 Pro (1502) lags due to slower recent gains; xAI and others face steeper scaling hurdles. Watch for Q2 announcements from major labs, as compute limits and benchmark saturation temper expectations for breakthrough jumps.

markets.question: Will Arvid Lindblad be the 2026 F1 Drivers' Champion?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the listed driver that finishes 1st in the driver standings for the 2026 F1 season.

This market will resolve as soon as the official results of the final scheduled race of the 2026 F1 season are known.

If multiple drivers tie for first place in the drivers standings, this market will resolve according to the tiebreak procedure used by F1 to determine the 2026 F1 Drivers’ champion.

If at any point it becomes impossible for a listed driver to win the 2026 F1 Drivers Championship based on the rules of F1 (e.g., they are mathematically eliminated from contention), the corresponding market will resolve to “No”.

If the F1 season is permanently canceled or has not been completed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from Formula 1.

eventMetadata.context_description: Kimi Antonelli's three consecutive victories in China, Japan, and the Miami Grand Prix—capped by his dominant pole-to-win performance last weekend—have propelled him to the top of the driver standings with 100 points, fueling trader consensus at 37% implied probability for the 2026 F1 Drivers' Championship. Teammate George Russell trails closely at 80 points with consistent podiums and the second-fastest Mercedes package, which leads the constructors' standings, keeping his 29.5% odds competitive amid their intra-team battle. Lando Norris sits third overall despite a strong Miami P2, but McLaren's pace lags Mercedes, while Max Verstappen's Red Bull struggles have dimmed his title hopes to 6.3%, underscoring the tight Mercedes duel with 16 races remaining and variable track demands ahead.

If the 2026 LCK CL season is postponed after September 20, 2026 11:59 PM ET, canceled, or a winner has not been declared in this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

If multiple teams are declared winner, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Riot Games (https://lolesports.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus prices the LCK Challengers League 2026 winner tightly among KT Rolster Challengers, HLE Challengers, SOOPers Challengers, FEARX Youth, and T1 Esports Academy at 27-33%, reflecting parity after Rounds 1-2 where Dplus Challengers and T1 lead the Challenge Group seeding at 8-2 series records, but recent Week 5 upsets expose vulnerabilities. SOOPers Challengers stunned leaders Dplus 2-0, KT fell 0-2 to KRX Challengers, and T1 notched a clean 2-0 over struggling HLE amid their five-match skid, injecting momentum into Trial Group contenders like FEARX on a two-win streak. With records carrying into group double round-robins and Bo5 playoffs ahead, academy depth from top LCK orgs fuels the volatile race.

markets.question: Will Gainare Tottori win on 2026-04-05?
markets.description:
In the upcoming game, scheduled for April 5, 2026
If Gainare Tottori wins, this market will resolve to "Yes".
Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".
If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed.
If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve "No".
This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Gainare Tottori vs. Reilac Shiga
events.description: This event is for the upcoming J2 100 Year Vision League game, scheduled for Sunday, April 5, 2026 between Gainare Tottori and Reilac Shiga.

eventMetadata.context_description: Gainare Tottori holds a slim edge as trader consensus favorite at home in the J2/J3 100 Year Vision League West B following their commanding 3-1 away win over Reilac Shiga on April 29, snapping a prior losing streak and highlighting defensive vulnerabilities in Shiga's backline during recent matches like a 2-0 loss to Sagan Tosu. Tottori's momentum from that head-to-head result, combined with Axis Bird Stadium home advantage, drives their 46.5% implied probability, while Shiga's mid-table position (7th) and struggles to keep clean sheets keep their away win viable at 37.5%. The tight odds reflect a closely contested matchup, with draw pricing underscoring frequent stalemates in prior encounters and both teams' inconsistent form. No major injury updates alter lineups.

markets.question: Counter-Strike: INOX Division vs Brute - Map 2 Winner
markets.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between INOX Division and Brute in the CCT Europe Challengers Series Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "INOX Division" if INOX Division win Map 2 against Brute.

This market will resolve to "Brute" if Brute win Map 2 against INOX Division.

If the match begins but is not completed, and Map 2 is concluded with a winner determined, this market will resolve based on the completed Map 2. 

If Map 2 is not completed for any reason, this market will resolve 50-50. 

If the match is canceled (not played at all) or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without play beginning, this market will resolve 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

events.title: Counter-Strike: INOX Division vs Brute (BO3) - CCT Europe Challengers Series Playoffs
events.description:
This market refers to the Counter-Strike Grand final match between INOX Division and Brute in the CCT Europe Challengers Series Playoffs, initially scheduled for May 10 at 10:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to "INOX Division" if INOX Division win the match against Brute.

This market will resolve to "Brute" if Brute win the match against INOX Division.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to the team who wins. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50. 

The resolution source for this market will be official information from https://hltv.org. However, if https://hltv.org has not published final results within 2 hours after the event’s conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead including video evidence.

In cases where a team’s listed name includes minor discrepancies from the resolution source, this market will resolve based on the underlying real-world match rather than exact name matching. Recognizable abbreviations, alternate or erroneous spellings, sponsor tags, affiliate or academy designations, regional identifiers, and minor formatting differences will be treated as referring to the same team, provided the intended team can be clearly and uniquely identified within the relevant competition. If a listed team name has no reasonable connection to any participating team, or if it matches or could reasonably refer to another team in the same competition such that the intended team cannot be unambiguously determined, this market will resolve 50-50.

eventMetadata.context_description: INOX Division holds a commanding edge in the CCT Europe Challengers Series 2 playoffs grand final BO3 against Brute, following a dominant 2-0 upper bracket final win yesterday—13-3 on Dust2 and 16-12 on Nuke—where FenomeN posted 2.04 rating and k0s excelled with 1.69 average. Ranked #87 with 85% win rate over three months and strong map pools like Ancient (86%) and Dust2 (82%), INOX remains undefeated in upper bracket play. Brute (#189, 43% win rate) rebounded from the loss via 2-1 lower bracket victory over Atreides today, seeking revenge, but weaker Dust2 (39%) and Nuke (29%) performances heighten upset risks in this double-elim decider amid roster consistency for both.

markets.question: Gold (XAUUSD) Up or Down on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Up" if the Close price for Gold (XAUUSD) on May 11, 2026 is higher than the Close price for Gold (XAUUSD) on the most recent prior trading day.

This market will resolve to "Down" if the Close price for Gold (XAUUSD) on May 11, 2026 is lower than the Close price for Gold (XAUUSD) on the most recent prior trading day.

E.g., ordinarily, a market on Monday would refer to the previous Friday for its most recent closing price, unless Friday were not a trading day under the applicable trading-hours schedule, in which case it would refer to the next most recent prior trading day.

If the two specified closing prices are exactly equal, this market will resolve 50-50. Closing prices will be used exactly as published by Pyth, without rounding.

If Gold (XAUUSD) does not trade at all during the relevant trading session, the market will resolve 50-50.

Trading days will be determined according to the applicable trading-hours schedule as listed on Pyth. Under the standard schedule, trading is open from 6:00:00 PM ET Sunday through 5:00:00 PM ET Friday, with a daily break from 5:00:00 PM ET to 6:00:00 PM ET, except where modified by holiday or special-session hours trading-hours as listed on Pyth. If a listed date is not a trading day under the applicable trading-hours schedule as listed on Pyth, this market will resolve 50-50.

For each trading day, the closing price refers to the Pyth "Close" value of the 1-minute candle timestamped 4:59 PM ET on that date.

If either of the relevant days has no valid Pyth Close value for the 1-minute candle timestamped 4:59 PM ET, the market will use the last valid Pyth price achieved prior to 4:59 PM ET during that trading day as the effective closing price. If no valid Pyth price exists for that trading day due to a system outage, data failure, or other technical disruption, the closing price for that day may be determined using the official daily close price of the CME COMEX Gold Futures (GC) futures contract for that trading day.

Only prices achieved during the applicable trading session will be considered.

In the event of a contract specification change, feed change, or similar structural modification affecting the market during the listed time frame, this market will resolve based on adjusted prices as displayed on Pyth.

The resolution source for this market will be Pyth, specifically the "Close" values for the relevant 1-minute candle available at https://pythdata.app/explore/Metal.XAU%2FUSD. Historical 1-minute candles may be accessed by appending a Unix timestamp (seconds) to the Pyth chart URL using the "t=" parameter.

markets.question: Will T1 make a roster change before July?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

The primary resolution source for this market will be Liquipedia; however a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if any player joins or leaves T1's active League of Legends roster (defined as the active lineup listed on the T1 Liquipedia page: https://liquipedia.net/leagueoflegends/T1) by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

As of market creation the active roster consists of the following players: Doran, Oner, Faker, Peyz, and Keria. Name changes or aliases referring to the same player do not qualify as roster changes.

For the purpose of this market, "roster change" refers to any official signing, transfer, benching to inactive/reserve, release, retirement, or departure of a player from the active lineup. This includes adding new players (transfers, free agent signings, or loans), removing players (to inactive, free agency, or other teams), or any change resulting in a different active roster.

Coach changes and temporary stand-ins for a single match will not count.

eventMetadata.context_description: T1's competitive 6-3 record in the LCK 2026 Spring Split, placing them third behind dominant Hanwha Life Esports and KT Rolster, balances trader sentiment on roster stability versus adjustment needs, with Yes at 53% reflecting Doran's contract expiring at season's end amid the team's recovery from an early 3-3 slump. Long-term deals—Faker to 2029, Oner and Peyz to 2028, Keria to 2029—anchor the core, reinforced by recent staff tweaks like Easyhoon's coaching addition following kkOma's temporary leave. Prolonged struggles or a shallow playoff exit could spur mid-split benching or top-lane swap before July's Summer lock; conversely, a deep run would solidify the lineup per T1's history of international peaks despite domestic inconsistencies.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus pins world No. 1 Aryna Sabalenka atop at 26.5% implied probability for the 2026 US Open title, buoyed by her commanding WTA rankings lead after the May 4 update following Madrid, where she showcased baseline power suited to hard courts. Elena Rybakina trails closely at 20.6%, reflecting her narrow points gap and semifinal run over Iga Swiatek at the Australian Open earlier this year, underscoring her flat-hitting prowess on faster surfaces. Swiatek's 16.5% accounts for clay-season focus potentially diluting her hard-court edge, while 19-year-old Victoria Mboko's surge to 11.5% stems from breakthrough deep runs at Indian Wells quarters and Miami quarters, injecting youth and upset potential into a wide-open draw with no outright dominator amid ongoing Italian Open clay volatility.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader sentiment on Polymarket heavily favors Alphabet as the 2nd largest company by market capitalization at May's end, with an 87.5% implied probability reflecting its current $4.81 trillion valuation trailing NVIDIA's $5.23 trillion lead by $420 billion. Alphabet's Q1 2026 earnings, released late April, showcased Google Cloud revenue surging 63% year-over-year to $20 billion amid robust AI infrastructure demand, propelling a 140% one-year stock gain and narrowing the gap to the top spot. NVIDIA carries a 9.3% chance of slipping to #2 if Alphabet sustains momentum, while Apple's 2.3% bid stems from its $4.31 trillion position and recent 2% daily uptick but faces a steeper climb. Key catalysts include NVIDIA's anticipated May 20 earnings and ongoing hyperscaler capital expenditures through month-end.

markets.question: Will Benoît Saint Denis fight Charles Oliveira next?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to face in a UFC bout.

Resolution for this market will be based on the next UFC fighter that Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight, regardless of whether the fight ends up taking place.

Only official announcements from the UFC, which include a scheduled date for the bout, will count. Announcements with no date or which do not confirm that the fight is official, speculation, or other unofficial announcements will not count.

If Charles Oliveira is officially announced to fight any non-listed fighter or no qualifying announcement is made by February 28, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the UFC.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus favors Dan Hooker at 38% implied probability for Charles Oliveira's next opponent following his unanimous decision win over Max Holloway to claim the BMF title at UFC 326 on March 7, but Arman Tsarukyan (31%) and Benoît Saint Denis (25%) keep the lightweight market tightly contested amid no official announcement. Hooker's edge stems from escalated post-fight trash talk, with Oliveira accusing him of fixation after Hooker's jabs at BMF pursuit, reigniting their rivalry. Tsarukyan's April callouts for a BMF clash—citing his prior victory—position him as a top-ranked contender alternative, while Saint Denis' recent BMF targeting adds momentum from his rising form. Oliveira's late-April eight-fight extension signals an imminent return, but UFC matchmaking uncertainty sustains the bunched odds among these stylistic striker-grappler matchups.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Toronto be 9°C on May 11?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station in degrees Celsius on 11 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Toronto Pearson Intl Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/ca/mississauga/CYYZ.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Les Wexner charged by June 30?
markets.description:

For the purposes of this market the District of Columbia and any county, municipality, or other subdivision of a State shall be included within the definition of a State.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from US governmental sources, however a wide consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket heavily favors no criminal charges against Les Wexner by June 30, reflecting the absence of any DOJ indictment or active federal prosecution despite heightened scrutiny. In February 2026, Wexner, the former L Brands CEO and Epstein's longtime financial patron, testified in a closed-door House Oversight Committee deposition, denying knowledge of Epstein's crimes, claiming he was duped and that Epstein stole vast sums from him—echoing prior statements without prompting charges. Recent civil lawsuits by Epstein victims in April 2026 allege Wexner enabled abuse through financial support, but these remain distinct from criminal proceedings. FBI documents listing him as a potential co-conspirator date to 2019 investigations that yielded no case, underscoring significant evidentiary or prosecutorial barriers amid his age (88) and expired statutes for many events.

markets.question: Iran leadership change by June 30?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the Supreme Leader of Iran, Mojtaba Khamenei, ceases to be the de facto leader of Iran at any point between market creation and the listed date (ET). Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

Mojtaba Khamenei will be considered to no longer be the de facto leader of Iran if he is removed from power, is detained, or otherwise loses his position or is prevented from acting as the de facto leader of Iran within this market's timeframe.

An official announcement of Mojtaba Khamenei’s resignation or removal will qualify for a "Yes" resolution regardless of when the announced departure goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket assigns roughly a 68% implied probability to no further change in Iran's supreme leadership by December 31, 2026, reflecting institutional continuity under Ayatollah Mojtaba Khamenei despite his severe injuries from U.S.-Israeli airstrikes in late February that killed his father, Ali Khamenei. Appointed in early March amid the 2026 Iran war, Mojtaba—now communicating via handwritten notes due to burns, leg surgeries, and speech difficulties—remains mentally sharp and directs key decisions, per senior officials, as the IRGC consolidates power during ceasefire talks that began April 8. Recent April reports highlight his seclusion for security, fueling speculation but no confirmed challenges; upcoming U.S.-Iran nuclear negotiations and truce extensions could test regime stability.

eventMetadata.context_description: Alex Fitzpatrick holds a one-shot lead at -14 over Kristoffer Reitan (-13) and two over Cameron Young (-12) in the final round of the Truist Championship at Quail Hollow Club, with leaders thru early afternoon holes and top 5 spots contested among Nicolai Højgaard, Sungjae Im (-10), and chasers Justin Thomas, Tommy Fleetwood, J.J. Spaun (-9). Pre-tournament withdrawals by Scottie Scheffler (rest for PGA Championship), Collin Morikawa (back injury), and Jake Knapp (thumb) thinned the elite field, while rain delays in rounds 1-3 softened the demanding par-71 layout favoring strong drivers and iron play. Fitzpatrick's third-round 64 and Young's 63 fueled trader consensus on their top 5 locks, but back-nine volatility and course history of late surges keep underdogs viable.

events.title: Cordoba: Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi vs Renzo Olivo
events.description:
This market refers to the tennis match between Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi and Renzo Olivo in the Cordoba, originally scheduled for May 11, 2026 at 9:00AM ET.

This market will resolve to 'Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi' if Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi advances against Renzo Olivo.

This market will resolve to 'Renzo Olivo' if Renzo Olivo advances against Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50.

If the match begins but is not completed, and one player advances due to the opponent's retirement, default, or disqualification, this market will resolve to the player who advances.

If the match ends in a walkover (player withdraws before the start and the other advances automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

The primary resolution source will be official information from the ATP Tour. A consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: In the ATP Challenger Cordoba round of 32 on outdoor clay, Luciano Emanuel Ambrogi (ATP 383) meets veteran compatriot Renzo Olivo (813, protected ranking) on May 11. Ambrogi enters with solid recent Challenger and ITF form, including quarterfinal runs on clay earlier in 2026, buoyed by his youth and two-handed backhand suited to the surface. Olivo, 34, leverages experience and a 1-0 head-to-head edge from their 2024 Buenos Aires clay win, but returns from a 2025 indefinite break with limited results. No injuries reported; home support and altitude could favor endurance, with trader consensus likely reflecting Ambrogi's ranking edge amid Olivo's upset potential.

markets.question: Will Lars Boje Mathiesen be the next prime minister of Denmark after the 2026 parliamentary elections?
markets.description:
A Parliamentary election is scheduled to be held in Denmark on March 24, 2026.

This market will resolve to the next individual who is officially appointed as Prime Minister of Denmark following the 2026 parliamentary election.

To count for resolution, the individual must be formally appointed as Prime Minister by the Danish monarch. Any interim or caretaker Prime Minister will not count toward the resolution of this market.

If no such Prime Minister is appointed by March 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to “Other”.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Government of Denmark; however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Following the March 24 snap parliamentary election, where Social Democrats secured 38 Folketing seats as the largest party but no bloc reached the 90-seat majority (red bloc 86, blue 78), coalition negotiations remain deadlocked. Trader consensus favors incumbent Prime Minister Mette Frederiksen at 60.5% implied probability due to her party's lead and potential for centrist pacts including Venstre and Moderates, per historical patterns. Recent May 8 developments—King Frederik X tasking Venstre leader and Deputy PM Troels Lund Poulsen as formateur after Frederiksen's talks collapsed—elevated his odds to 19.6%, with Moderates' Lars Løkke Rasmussen at 14.1% as pivotal kingmaker. Talks resume May 11 amid cross-party maneuvering.

markets.question: Will "Bugonia" be the top US Netflix movie this week?
markets.description:
Netflix is expected to update its Top 10 Movies list on top10.netflix.com on Tuesday, May 12, 2026, 3:00 PM ET, reflecting viewership from the previous week (Monday to Sunday).

This market will resolve based on which movie this update ranks as the #1 Netflix movie in the United States.

The ranking is based on total views in the United States, as reported by Netflix for movies.

If the top10.netflix.com update does not occur by May 15, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The body-swap comedy, featuring voices from Michael B. Jordan, Juno Temple, and Tracy Morgan, has sustained strong daily rankings—leading on FlixPatrol and holding #2 on Tudum amid high family viewership despite a modest 69-71% Rotten Tomatoes score, where audiences outperform critics. Its chart trajectory reflects robust streaming metrics and promotional buzz outpacing holdovers. Realistic upsets hinge on Apex's action-thriller binge momentum in final days or a surprise surge from Roommates or Green Book, though thin competition leaves little room for shifts before weekly lock.

markets.question: Will Elon Musk post 840-879 tweets in May 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the number of times Elon Musk (@elonmusk), posts on X during the month of May 2026.

For the purposes of this market, only main feed posts, quote posts and reposts will count.

Replies will NOT count towards the total - however, replies on the main feed such as https://x.com/elonmusk/status/1786073478711353576 will be counted by the tracker.

Deleted posts will count as long as they remain available long enough to be captured by the tracker (~5 minutes).

Community reposts which are not counted by the tracker not count toward the total.

The resolution source for this market is the 'Post Counter' figure for posts found at https://xtracker.polymarket.com. Individual posts can be viewed by clicking "Export Data". If the tracker does not update correctly in accordance with the rules, X itself may be used as a secondary resolution source.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket's Elon Musk May 2026 X posts market remains tightly fragmented across 700-879 bins, with 800-839 edging at 9.8% implied probability amid his early-month slowdown to ~16 posts daily through May 2 due to OpenAI trial court appearances, followed by a 48-post surge on May 3 and stabilization at 18/day per trackers as of May 7. This projects a full-month pace of 22-27 posts daily—below March's 42/day peak but above recent weekly resolutions like April 24-May 1's ~200—hinging on rebound potential from Tesla/SpaceX updates or political viral moments. Key swing factors include daily engagement spikes from platform announcements or controversies, with no dominant catalyst yet breaking the deadlock among mid-700s contenders.

markets.question: Will Paloma Valencia place 2nd in the 1st round of the 2026 Colombian presidential election?
markets.description:
Colombia's presidential elections are scheduled for May 31, 2026, and a second round (if required) on June 21, 2026, in case no candidate secures more than 50% of the valid votes in the first round.

This market will resolve according to the listed candidate who receives the second-most valid votes in the first round of this election.

The named candidates will be primarily ranked by the number of valid votes received in the specified election. If two or more candidates are tied on valid votes, ties will be broken by alphabetical order of the candidates' last names. This market will resolve to the candidate that occupies the second-highest finishing position after applying this ranking.

If the results of the first round of the Colombian presidential election are not known by December 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

This market will resolve based on the election results, as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by Colombia's National Civil Registry (Registraduría Nacional del Estado Civil) (https://registraduria.gov.co).

eventMetadata.context_description: Recent Invamer and AtlasIntel polls through early May position Abelardo de la Espriella as the clear second-place contender behind Iván Cepeda in Colombia's May 31 first-round presidential election, with de la Espriella polling 21-31% to Paloma Valencia's 19-23% and Cepeda's leading 35-44%. Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects this polling edge, where de la Espriella's momentum in daily tracking data has widened his lead over Valencia amid right-wing vote consolidation post-primaries. Cepeda's lower odds for second stem from his frontrunner status, though a fragmented field leaves room for shifts before the runoff-triggering threshold of 50% plus one vote. Pre-election violence and final debates could sway undecideds in battleground regions.

markets.question: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino: Both Teams to Score
markets.description:
In the upcoming Brazil Série A game between Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET:

This market will resolve to "Yes" if both Santos FC and Red Bull Bragantino each score at least one goal during the game.

This market will resolve to "No" if either team fails to score (i.e., if one or both teams finish with zero goals).

If the game is postponed, this market will remain open until the game has been completed. If the game is canceled entirely, with no make-up game, this market will resolve 50–50.

If the game is started but not completed, this market will resolve according to the official final score published on cbf.com.br. This market refers only to the outcome within the first 90 minutes of regular play plus stoppage time.

The primary resolution source for this market is the official statistics of the event as recognized by the governing body or event organizers. However, if the governing body or event organizers have not published final match statistics within 2 hours after the event's conclusion, a consensus of credible reporting may be used instead.

events.title: Santos FC vs. Red Bull Bragantino - More Markets
events.description: More markets for the Brazil Série A game, scheduled for May 10 at 5:30 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Santos FC, languishing in 18th place in the 2026 Brasileirão Série A after a dismal 1-5-5 start with just 8 points and a -7 goal difference through 11 matches, hosts mid-table Red Bull Bragantino (9th, 4-2-5, 14 points, +1 GD) at Estádio Urbano Caldeira on May 10. Santos desperately needs points in their relegation scrap, bolstered by home advantage and competitive head-to-head history—including a 2-2 draw last September—but faces injury woes with right-back Mayke (knee), midfielder Gabriel Menino (hamstring), and uncertainty over star forward Neymar following March muscle fatigue that sidelined him briefly. Bragantino contends with absences up front, including strikers Eduardo Sasha and Fernando (both ankle), potentially exposing defensive vulnerabilities on the road amid Santos' urgent momentum shift.

events.title: FC Barcelona vs. Real Madrid CF - Player Props
events.description: Goal scorers for the La Liga game between FC Barcelona and Real Madrid CF, scheduled for May 10, 2026 at 3:00 PM ET.

eventMetadata.context_description: Barcelona hold a commanding nine-point lead atop the La Liga table entering this pivotal El Clásico at Spotify Camp Nou, where a victory on May 10 could clinch the title with three matchdays remaining. Recent injury blows loom large: Lamine Yamal is out for Barcelona, sidelining their third-leading scorer (16 goals) and boosting opportunities for Robert Lewandowski (13 goals) and Raphinha, who is easing back from hamstring issues; Real Madrid face doubts over Kylian Mbappé's hamstring (La Liga's top scorer with 24 goals), plus confirmed absences of Dani Carvajal (toe fracture) and Ferland Mendy, shifting reliance to Vinícius Júnior (15 goals) amid defensive vulnerabilities. Barcelona's strong recent form—six wins in seven—contrasts Madrid's need for a statement result, heightening stakes for player props on goals, assists, and shots in this high-intensity rivalry fixture.

markets.question: Will EUR/USD hit 1.35 (High) in 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to “Yes” if the Investing.com high price (“H”) for any EUR/USD hourly candle for an hour on or before the listed end date (ET) is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

Data for a given candle will be considered finalized once the next candle appears on the specified graph. The last trading day of a given week will be considered finalized once the market closes on that day, typically at 5 PM ET on Friday.

This market will resolve as soon as any finalized EUR/USD hourly candle high price is equal to or above the listed price, or once the final hourly candle in the specified period is finalized. A candle starting at 11:00 PM ET on a given date will be considered to be on that date.

This market’s resolution will be based solely on information from the “H” figure located at the top of the EUR/USD Streaming Chart on Investing.com for the specified currency pair (e.g., https://www.investing.com/currencies/eur-usd-chart).

eventMetadata.context_description: EUR/USD trades near 1.178 as of May 10, 2026, reflecting short-term dollar softening after mixed U.S. data, but persistent Federal Reserve-European Central Bank policy divergence—Fed funds at 3.50%-3.75% versus ECB deposit rate at 2.00%—bolsters trader consensus for USD strength through year-end. ECB's April 30 decision to hold rates amid projected Eurozone HICP inflation of 2.6% for 2026 underscores sticky price pressures and modest 1.0% GDP growth forecasts, contrasting resilient U.S. expansion. Key swing factors include June 16-17 FOMC meeting rate projections and July ECB policy review; a break above 1.1850 resistance could signal euro upside, while sub-1.1700 support eyes deeper retracement on hawkish Fed signals.

markets.question: Will the median home value in the DC Metro area be between $566,000 and $572,000 on May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the median home value for all property types in the Washington, D.C. Metro area on May 31, 2026.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

The resolution source will be official data from the Parcl Labs Sales Price Index for the Washington, D.C. Metro area (Parcl_ID: 2900475). The settlement price will be calculated by multiplying the published price index value (price per square foot) by 1800 square feet, which is the median home size in the Washington, D.C. Metro area. Parcl is set to publish this data on May 31, 2026. If no data for May 31 is released by June 10, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve according to the most recently published data. (see: https://app.parcllabs.com/prediction-market-resolutions/36)

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Los Angeles be between 66-67°F on May 12?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the temperature range that contains the highest temperature recorded at the Los Angeles International Airport Station in degrees Fahrenheit on 12 May '26.

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Los Angeles International Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/us/ca/los-angeles/KLAX.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Fahrenheit (eg, 21°F). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects tight uncertainty in National Weather Service guidance for Los Angeles' highest temperature on May 12, with ensemble models like GFS and ECMWF clustering around 66-70°F amid persistent marine layer and onshore flow. The National Weather Service Los Angeles/Oxnard forecast calls for a Tuesday high near 66°F under mostly sunny skies after morning clouds, driven by strengthening westerly winds advecting cool Pacific air that suppresses daytime heating. Slight model spread stems from potential high-pressure ridging aloft allowing earlier stratus clearing and temperatures edging toward 70°F, as seen in yesterday's 69°F maximum at LAX. Climatological May normals hover at 69°F, with key resolution hinging on observed peak at KLAX; watch Monday evening's updated forecast discussion for refined onshore flow trends.

markets.question: Will gas hit (High) $4.60 by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if on any day between market creation and May 31, 2026, the average US regular gas price is equal to or above the listed price. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Only the first two decimal digits of the reported price will be considered (e.g., if the price is reported as $3.257, this market will use $3.25 as the price).

The resolution source for this market will be information from the American Automobile Association (AAA), presently found here: https://gasprices.aaa.com/. Specifically, the cell under "Regular" and for the row "Current Avg".

The EIA's latest Weekly Petroleum Status Report shows crude stocks down sharply for the week ending May 1, with gasoline production dipping, amplifying refinery margin pressures as summer driving demand ramps up ahead of Memorial Day. Polymarket traders price a 62-75% implied probability of breaching $4.60 by May 31, reflecting consensus on sustained upside from geopolitical risks and OPEC+ dynamics, though EIA's STEO forecasts a softer annual average of $3.70. markets.question: Will Steve Witkoff attend the next US x Iran diplomatic meeting?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if the listed individual attends the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran by June 30, 2026, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to “No”.

A diplomatic meeting refers to a deliberate meeting between representatives of the listed countries who are acting in an official capacity and are authorized to engage in negotiation or diplomacy regarding US-Iranian relations on behalf of their governments. Meetings conducted indirectly, for example, through designated mediators, facilitators, or interlocutors acting with the knowledge and authorization of the relevant governments, will qualify.

Brief greetings, chance encounters, or talks otherwise not deliberately aimed at diplomacy or negotiation will not qualify as diplomatic meetings.

The meeting must be in-person (including indirect in-person meetings) and must be publicly acknowledged by either government or reported by a consensus of credible media. Remote meetings, phone calls, or other meetings where the relevant parties are not present will not count.

Attendance refers to the listed individual being physically present and actively participating in negotiations at the meeting.

If the next diplomatic meeting between representatives of the United States and Iran takes place over multiple days, attendance at any part of the meeting will qualify.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the listed individual and the governments of the United States and Iran; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: US strikes on Iranian-linked tankers in the Strait of Hormuz two days ago have intensified tensions, even as the Trump administration awaits Tehran's response to a ceasefire proposal offering a 60-day truce, nuclear enrichment freeze, sanctions relief, and waterway reopening. The last direct talks in Islamabad, Pakistan, on April 11—led by US Vice President JD Vance, Special Envoy Steve Witkoff, and Jared Kushner opposite Iranian Foreign Minister Abbas Araghchi and Parliament Speaker Mohammad Bagher Ghalibaf—failed after 21 hours without resolution on core issues like Iran's nuclear program and ballistic missiles. Mediators Qatar, Pakistan, Oman, Saudi Arabia, Egypt, and Turkey now coordinate indirect diplomacy, with Secretary of State Marco Rubio and Witkoff recently engaging Qatar's prime minister. Iran's delayed reply and IRGC influence heighten uncertainty over the next meeting's timing, venue, and senior attendees.

markets.question: Will Movimento Democrático Brasileiro (MDB) win the most seats in the next Brazilian Senate election?
markets.description:

Only seats contested in the next Brazilian Senate election will be considered.

markets.description:

This market will resolve to "Anyone's Legend" if Anyone's Legend secures 11 or more kills than Weibo Gaming in the match.

If the match is canceled (not played at all), ends in a tie, or is delayed beyond 7 days from the scheduled date without a winner determined, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match begins but is not completed, and one team wins due to the opponent's forfeiture, disqualification, or walkover, this market will resolve to 50-50. 

If the match ends in a forfeit, disqualification, or walkover (team withdraws before the start and the other wins automatically), this market will resolve to 50-50.

markets.question: Will the highest temperature in Madrid be 15°C or below on May 11?

The resolution source for this market will be information from Wunderground, specifically the highest temperature recorded for all times on this day by the Forecast for the Adolfo Suárez Madrid-Barajas Airport Station once information is finalized, available here: https://www.wunderground.com/history/daily/es/madrid/LEMD.

To toggle between Fahrenheit and Celsius, click the gear icon next to the search bar and switch the Temperature setting between °F and °C.

This market can not resolve to "Yes" until all data for this date has been finalized.

The resolution source for this market measures temperatures to whole degrees Celsius (eg, 9°C). Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

Any revisions to temperatures recorded after data is finalized for this market's timeframe will not be considered for this market's resolution.

markets.question: Decibel FDV above $200M one day after launch?

​A candidate's party will be determined by their ballot-listed or otherwise identifiable affiliation with that party at the time all of the 2026 House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources. A candidate without a ballot-listed affiliation to either the Democrat or Republican parties will be considered a member of one of these parties based on the party with which they most recently expressed their intent to caucus at the time all of the House elections are conclusively called by this market's resolution sources.

This market will resolve based on the result of the election as indicated by a consensus of credible reporting. If there is ambiguity, this market will resolve based solely on the official results as reported by the United States government, specifically the Federal Election Commission (https://www.fec.gov/).

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus heavily favors the Republican Party at 84% implied probability to win Wisconsin's 5th Congressional District House seat, reflecting Rep. Scott Fitzgerald's incumbency advantage in a district rated Solid Republican by the Cook Political Report with an R+11 partisan lean. Fitzgerald won his last general election by 64.5% and faces no serious primary challengers ahead of the June 1 filing deadline and August 11 primaries. Democrats field Andrew Beck and Ben Steinhoff in their primary, but both lack the name recognition or fundraising to threaten a flip in this suburban Milwaukee stronghold, despite slight leftward trends in recent cycles. No recent polling or developments have shifted the dynamics, underscoring structural barriers to a Democratic upset absent a major national wave or scandal.

markets.question: Another GTA VI trailer released by May 31?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if another official trailer for the next GTA (GTA VI) is released by Rockstar Games or any of its official accounts, including but not limited to Rockstar's official website, YouTube channel, Twitter, or any other official social media platform between market creation and May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET.

An "official trailer" is defined as a video clearly labelled and marketed as a trailer for the game in question. Teasers, gameplay snippets, or other promotional materials will not qualify for resolution unless explicitly marketed as an official trailer by Rockstar Games.

This market will resolve immediately if such an official trailer is released by the expiry date. If no official trailer is released by the specified date, this market will resolve to "No".

events.description:

An "official trailer" is defined as a video clearly labelled and marketed as a trailer for the game in question. Teasers, gameplay snippets, or other promotional materials will not qualify for resolution unless explicitly marketed as an official trailer by Rockstar Games.

This market will resolve immediately if such an official trailer is released by the expiry date. If no official trailer is released by the specified date, this market will resolve to "No".

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket reflects a 67.5% implied probability for a third GTA VI trailer before May 31, driven by the game's confirmed May 26 release date—just weeks away—and Rockstar Games' historical pattern of ramping up marketing with multiple trailers ahead of launch, as seen with GTA V. Speculation intensified after Take-Two Interactive scheduled its Q4 fiscal 2026 earnings call for May 21, where GTA VI updates are anticipated, echoing last year's surprise Trailer 2 drop post-delay announcement on May 6, 2025. While no official word has emerged, fan theories citing release proximity and one-year anniversaries have boosted sentiment, though Rockstar's secrecy leaves room for a pre-order push or shadow drop as key catalysts.

markets.question: Will Morgan Ensberg be the next Red Sox manager?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the person who is appointed as the next permanent manager of the Boston Red Sox.

If no permanent manager is appointed by January 31, 2027, 11:59 PM ET, the market will resolve to "Other."

Appointments of 'interim,' 'caretaker,' or other non-permanent managers will not impact this market's resolution.

An announcement of a new permanent manager's appointment before this market's close date will immediately resolve this market to the corresponding option, regardless of when the announced appointment goes into effect.

The primary resolution source for this market will be official information from the Boston Red Sox; however, a consensus of credible reporting will also be used.

markets.question: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by December 31, 2026?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sanae Takaichi meets with Xi Jinping between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Takaichi and Jinping are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

events.title: Will Xi meet with Takaichi by...?
events.description:
This market will resolve to "Yes" if Sanae Takaichi meets with Xi Jinping between market creation and the listed date, 11:59 PM ET. Otherwise, this market will resolve to "No".

A meeting is defined as any encounter where both Takaichi and Jinping are present and interact with each other in person.

An exchange of words, handshake, direct conversation, or other clear personal interaction between the named individuals will qualify as a meeting. Merely standing in proximity, making eye contact, or being present in the same room or event without direct interaction will not qualify.

The resolution source will be a consensus of credible reporting.

eventMetadata.context_description: Japanese Prime Minister Sanae Takaichi, reelected in a February 2026 landslide that solidified her hawkish stance on China, has not met President Xi Jinping since their October 2025 APEC summit in South Korea, where they discussed stable ties amid rare earth export controls and Senkaku Islands tensions. Ongoing 2025-2026 China-Japan diplomatic strains, exacerbated by Tokyo's defense buildup and closer U.S. alignment—including recent talks with Treasury Secretary Scott Bessent—have cooled bilateral diplomacy. Traders weigh prospects for a pre-December 31, 2026, summit at potential forums like the G20 or APEC, against risks from territorial disputes, Beijing's export restrictions, and Takaichi's "Japan First" Indo-Pacific policy, reflecting uncertain de-escalation signals.

markets.question: Will Golden Lions win LRS 2026 Split 1?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the winner of the Liga Regional Sur (LRS) 2026 Split 1.

If the 2026 Split 1 is postponed after May 16, 2026 11:59 PM ET, canceled, or a winner has not been declared in this timeframe, this market will resolve to "Other".

If multiple teams are declared winner, this market will resolve in favor of the team whose listed team name comes first alphabetically.

The resolution source for this market will be official information from Riot Games (https://lolesports.com/); however, a consensus of credible reporting may also be used.

eventMetadata.context_description: Volticons and Golden Lions share trader consensus at around 44% implied probabilities for the LRS 2026 Split 1 title, reflecting their unbeaten runs through upper bracket playoffs—Golden Lions' dominant 3-0 sweep of Seven Dark on April 21 and Volticons' gritty 3-2 comeback over second-place Malvinas Gaming on April 28—setting up a decisive Bo5 upper bracket final. Regular season dominance underpinned this, with Golden Lions topping at 6-1 series (13-3 games) and Volticons close behind at 5-2 (12-5 games) on a five-match win streak, capped by their pivotal 2-1 head-to-head victory in Week 6. Malvinas lingers at 12% after the playoff drop, while lower bracket action like 9z Team's 3-0 win over Seven Dark on May 5 keeps paths open but favors the upper bracket leaders in this tight race.

markets.question: Will Kaitlin Purrington be the Republican nominee for AZ-01?
markets.description:
This market will resolve according to the candidate who wins the nomination for the Republican Party to contest the AZ-01 congressional district seat in the U.S. House of Representatives in the 2026 midterm elections. The Republican primary will take place on August 4, 2026.

If no nominee is announced by November 3, 2026, 11:59PM ET, this market will resolve to "Other".

The resolution source for this market will be a consensus of official Republican sources, including https://www.rnc.org/.

Any replacement of the nominee before election day will not change the resolution of the market.

eventMetadata.context_description: Jay Feely's commanding 70.5% implied probability in the AZ-01 Republican primary stems from his dominant first-quarter fundraising haul exceeding $740,000, Trump endorsement, and backing from House GOP leadership including Speaker Mike Johnson, positioning him to consolidate support after rival Gina Swoboda switched to the secretary of state race in February. State Rep. Joseph Chaplik, at 17.8%, draws grassroots appeal as a district resident with a proven conservative legislative record in Scottsdale's LD3, bolstered by an April poll showing him ahead amid high undecideds, though traders prioritize Feely's resources ahead of the July 21 primary. A May 5 PBS debate between Feely and John Trobough highlighted attacks on Feely as a carpetbagger, but failed to dent his momentum in this open seat race vacated by David Schweikert's gubernatorial bid.

markets.question: Will a dozen eggs cost between $2.75–3.00 in April?
markets.description:
This market will resolve to the bracket within which the price for "Eggs, Grade A, Large (Cost per Dozen) in U.S. City Average" lies when the April data point is published by the St. Louis Fed (https://fred.stlouisfed.org/series/APU0000708111).

The St. Louis Fed bases its numbers for egg prices on the BLS's CPI release. The April release is presently scheduled for May 12, 2026. Resolution of this market will take place upon the update of the St. Louis Fed's chart. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

The resolution source for this market measures prices to the third decimal place. Thus, this is the level of precision that will be used when resolving the market.

If the reported value falls exactly between two brackets, then this market will resolve to the higher range bracket.

events.description:

The St. Louis Fed bases its numbers for egg prices on the BLS's CPI release. The April release is presently scheduled for May 12, 2026. If no data for the specified month is released by the date the next month's data is scheduled to be released, this market will resolve based on data from the last available month.

eventMetadata.context_description: Trader consensus on Polymarket prices the $2.25–2.50/dozen range for the U.S. Bureau of Labor Statistics (BLS) average retail price of grade A large eggs in April 2026 at a 66% implied probability, reflecting sustained supply recovery from 2025 avian influenza outbreaks that had driven prices above $6/dozen. March 2026 BLS data confirmed $2.348/dozen, down 6.2% from February's $2.50, as laying hen inventories rebounded amid fewer highly pathogenic H5N1 cases, boosting production and easing wholesale pressures. The $2.00–2.25 bin at 23.5% captures expectations of further moderation per USDA forecasts toward $2.16 later in 2026, tempered by seasonal Easter demand in early April. Resolution awaits the BLS April release on May 12.

markets.question: Will Starmer say "Liz" or "Truss" during the next Prime Minister's Questions event?
markets.description:
Keir Starmer is scheduled to participate in the Prime Minister's Questions (PMQs) in the Commons Chamber in the Palace of Westminster on every Wednesday. (https://www.parliament.uk/visiting/visiting-and-tours/watch-committees-and-debates/prime-ministers-questions/).

This market will resolve to "Yes" if Keir Starmer says the listed term during the next Prime Minister's Question Time event he participates in. Otherwise, the market will resolve to "No".

Any usage of the term regardless of context will count toward the resolution of this market.

Pluralization/possessive of the term will count toward the resolution of this market, however other forms will NOT count.

Instances where the term is used in a compound word will count regardless of context (e.g. joyful is not a compound word for "joy," however "killjoy" is a compounding of the words "kill" and "joy").

If this market requires a specified number of mentions of a person’s first or last name, a full-name mention will count as one mention (e.g., if a market is about “Joe / Biden 5+ times,” a mention of “Joe Biden” will count once).

AI-generated audio or video will not count toward this market's resolution.

If no such Prime Minister's Question Time event happens by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No". If Starmer ceases to be Prime Minister for any length of time by May 31, 2026, 11:59 PM ET, this market will resolve to "No".

This market is explicitly about scheduled events featuring Keir Starmer titled "Prime Minister's Question Time" (https://www.parallelparliament.co.uk/mp/keir-starmer/dept/cabinet-office/calendar). Speeches, events, or comments that occur outside of named, scheduled events will not qualify toward this market's resolution. If the event contains a Q&A, it will count toward the resolution of this market.

The resolution source will be video of the events. Only remarks which are broadcast or streamed live will count toward this market's resolution.

eventMetadata.context_description: Labour's severe losses in the May 7 local elections, where Reform UK gained over 1,400 seats and control of multiple councils while Greens split the left-wing vote, have triggered internal revolt including a backbench MP's demand for cabinet ministers to back a new leader or face a leadership challenge. Prime Minister Keir Starmer, vowing to fight on, faces amplified scrutiny at the next Prime Minister's Questions on May 13 amid the State Opening of Parliament marking a new session. Opposition leader Kemi Badenoch is set to target electoral defeats, welfare clashes, economic stagnation, and Peter Mandelson's delayed Foreign Office vetting tied to past ties. Trader consensus reflects Starmer's established PMQs rhetoric patterns against these catalysts, with outcomes verified via official Commons video and Hansard transcripts if he participates as Prime Minister.
